phase1.openingDialogue: Know your enemy! Let's learn the most common exceptions~ 🐛

phase2.explanation:
Conceptual lesson — no broken code!

Common misconception: "NullPointerException means the variable is undefined."

Actually: null means the variable has no object reference. The variable exists, but doesn't point to any object.

String str = null;  // str exists, but points to nothing
str.length();       // NPE because null doesn't have a length

Fix: Initialize your variables properly!

Another misconception: "IOException should be ignored."

Actually: I/O errors happen frequently (file not found, disk full, permission denied). Always handle them gracefully with meaningful user feedback.

phase3.prompt:
Which exception would be thrown by the following code?

String[] names = {"Alice", "Bob"};
System.out.println(names[2]);

A) NullPointerException
B) ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
C) NumberFormatException
D) ClassCastException
E) ArithmeticException

Select the correct exception.
dialogueHints: Look at the array declaration — how many elements does it have?
What happens when you try to access an index that doesn't exist?
phase3.openingDialogue: Quick quiz! Which exception is this? 🤔
